title: persoonia muelleri subsp. angustifolia
intro:
Persoonia muelleri subsp. angustifolia, also known as a narrow-leaved geebung, is a plant species native to eastern Australia, characterized by its small, green, and waxy leaves, and its clusters of small, white flowers.

It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to help reduce pain and swelling, as well as promote wound healing. The health benefits of Persoonia muelleri subsp. angustifolia include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, and its anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms of eczema and other skin irritations.

Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used externally to treat skin conditions or applied topically to affected areas.
